calendar.matters:
ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-22-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Richard Koch for Mr. Brinkley. Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara de Vries (Former Trustee).

Mr. Koch stated that he has a statement by Mr. Brinkley stating that he has closed the account ending in #8395 because the service charge was too high and his new account number is ending with #3159. Mr. Koch reported that the account is in Mr. Brinkley's name. The Court is satisfiled and the status hearing is taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Sr. Deputy M. Benton.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-22-2013 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-22-2013 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-18-2012 Continued To Jan-22-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Richard Koch to appear on January 22, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why Mr. Brinkley has not signed the reciept of funds. The status hearings were also continued to date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-18-2012 Continued To Jan-22-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-13-2012 Continued To Dec-18-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to Mr. Koch to appear on December 18, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-08-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Terminate Trust.
Off Calendar Jun-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara DeVries (Trustee). Richard Koch for Robert Brinkley (Petitioner).

The Court stated that the matter appeared to be in order at this point to terminate the Trust and took the issue of Mr. Koch's fees under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Bui.

Petition To Terminate Trust.
Off Calendar May-04-2011 Continued To Jun-08-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch for Robbie Brinkley.

Mr. Koch stated that Mr. Brinkley was not feeling well and that Mr. Brinkley asked that the matter be put over for one week. The Court informed Mr. Koch that the supplement to the Petition to Terminate the Trust that he filed was a fee request and that it was not appropriate, and that it needed verification from Mr. Brinkley. Mr. Koch stated that there was no new Trust, that there is Wells Fargo Management Services, that there was not enough money to establish a Trust and that he filed the supplement to avoid filing fees. In addition, Mr. Koch stated that the funds were transferred to the Wells Fargo account, that the will was not finalized and that he has not been told who the Beneficiary is. The Petition to Terminate the Special Needs Trust was continued to June 8, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard at the same time of Ms. Alavi's petition.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Terminate Trust.
Off Calendar Apr-20-2011 Continued To May-04-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Terminate Trust.
Off Calendar Feb-09-2011 Continued To Apr-20-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch for and with Robbie Brinkley. Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara DeVries.

Mr. Koch and Mr. Brinkley presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court will grant the petition to terminate the Trust subject to Mr. Siracusa's filing of a final account, the money going into Wells Fargo, and filing of fee petitions for Mr. Siracusa, Ms. DeVries and Mr. Koch. The matter was continued to April 20, 2011 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jan-05-2011 Off Calendar

Petition To Terminate Trust.
Off Calendar Jan-05-2011 Continued To Feb-09-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Barbara DeVries. Richard Koch for and with Robert Brinkley.

Mr. Koch stated that $81,658.00 was used for the deposit on the property, that $11,985.00 was used for electrical upgrades, that $10,400.00 was used for the furnace/heating and that escrow had closed. In addition, Mr. Koch stated Mr. Brinkely was residing in the new property, that he was working on the Living Trust, that he expected to complete the Trust when the termination issue of the Special Needs Trust is settled and that he would file it in Marin County where the property is located. Ms. Alavi stated that the revocable Trust needed to be established in order to transfer the asset and that Special Needs Trust should not be terminated. Mr. Koch's fees are to be addressed when the petition for final account is filed. Mr. Koch informed the Court that the property was held in Mr. Brinkley's name not the Special Needs Trust, stated that Mr. Brinkley no longer wanted Ms. DeVries to be Co-Trustee of the Trust and that Mr. Brinkley wanted others to assist him. The Court stated that having a Professional Fiduciary as Co-Trustee with Mr. Brinkley provides protection for him. Mr. Koch will look into Ms. Sylvester's status as a Professional Fiduciary. The matter was continued to February 9, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the revocable Trust and the Co-Trustee being a professional fiduciary.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Terminate Trust.
Off Calendar Nov-10-2010 Continued To Jan-05-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Barbara DeVries (Trustee). Richard Koch for and with Robert Brinkley. Dr. Tara Collins (Mr. Brinkley's Physician) in Propria Persona.

The Court read Dr. Collins' letter dated November 4, 2010 which was filed on November 8, 2010 and Ms. DeVries' declaration. The Court expressed concern about Mr. Brinkley's exposure to others and the possiblity of being taken advantage of, and not having something in place to help protect his assets. The Court suggested an Inter-Vivos Trust. Mr. Brinkley stated that he would like to continue to have help from Ms. DeVries. Discussions were held with regard to Mr. Brinkley and Ms. DeVries being Co-Trustees. Mr. Schultz stated that he would file the Fourth Account and requested a status date for filing of the Fifth and Final Account. Mr. Koch is to file his petition for fees. Mr. Schultz will file an Ex Parte request for a distribution in the amount of $100,000.00 for an increase of the down payment. The Petition to Terminate the Special Needs Trust and the future accounting was continued to January 5, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 status of the 5th and Final Account and for status of the conversion of the Trust. The status report/account of the $50,000.00 was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Anasse.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-10-2010 Continued To Jan-05-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Report/Account Of The $50,000.00 By Mr. Brinkley
Off Calendar Nov-10-2010 Off Calendar

Petition To Terminate Trust.
Off Calendar Oct-19-2010 Continued To Nov-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch for and with Robert Brinkley. Lawrence Siracusa for and with Barbara DeVries.

The Court expressed concern that the $50,000.00 released to Mr. Brinkley for the purpose of seeing if he could manage funds and pay bills, instead was used to enter into a contract for the purchase of real estate. Mr. Brinkley stated that he opened a checking account and that some funds were spent on additional inspections and estimates on the property. Mr. Siracusa stated that his Client stated that Mr. Brinkley does manage his monthly income, that she did not know about about the offer on the house, that there was concern but that nothing has occurred for filing of an objection, that Mr. Brinkley was doing very well and that his Client felt that he could live on his own. The Court stated that it did not contemplate Mr. Brinkley putting a deposit on a house or a change in his living arrangement. The matter was continued to November 10, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. DeVries to obtain a statement from Mr. Brinkley's current Physician regarding his ability to handle funds and for Ms. DeVries to work with Mr. Brinkley to determine if purchase of the property is in his best interest. Mr. Koch stated that the sale was extended for 30 days.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5220. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-18-2010 Continued To Oct-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Terminate Trust.
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Continued To Oct-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Barbara DeVries (Trustee). Richard Koch for and with Robbie Brinkley.

Mr. Koch stated that Mr. Brinkley never received public services from the Dept. of Health Care Recovery and owes nothing, that all expenses were paid by the Veterans Administration, that his Client's correct name was Robbie not Robert, and that Robbie was his birth name. Ms. Alavi stated that they were not opposing termination of the Trust and requested a retainer in the amount of $20,000.00 for Court costs and legal fees. Mr. Brinkley stated that he wanted to manage his own money and answered a series of questions presented by the Court. The Court informed Mr. Koch that any fees incurred were subject to Court review, stated that it was not satisfied with the letter received from the Physician and expressed concern on Mr. Brinkley's behalf. The Court will allow that $50,000.00 be paid to Mr. Brinkley and have a status report in six months to see how things go. Mr. Koch's fees are to be submitted by way of a petition. The matter was continued to October 19, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a status report on management of the funds.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5520. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Bloom.

Off Calendar Jun-23-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-27-2009 Continued To Jun-15-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch for and with Robbie Brinkley. Avid Alavi for Barbara De Vries.

Ms. Alavi stated that she filed the Second Amended Account to address Mr. Koch's fees and for guidance as to the reimbursement of the $77,760.00 cost for dental work. In addition, Ms. Alavi informed the Court that the appeal with the VA was still pending. Mr. Brinkley stated that he wanted Mr. Koch to represent him and that he wanted the Court to approve payment of his attorney's fees. Mr. Koch explained the extent of services he provided. The Court stated that payment of the dental work should come out of the Special Needs Trust and that there was an issue with Mr. Koch's time sheets. Mr. Koch will submit a supplement to support his request for fees by May 25, 2009. The matter was continued to June 15, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and will be taken under submission on that date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Sum.
